name: Japan Open
stakes: This Super 750 event offers crucial ranking points and a vital platform for India's top shuttlers to solidify their form and build momentum ahead of the season's major championships.
summary:
India arrives at the Japan Open, a Super 750 event, with a mix of established stars and promising talents eager to make their mark. Leading the charge are the formidable men's doubles duo, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty, who are fresh off a dominant Singapore Open title run where they defeated the reigning world champions. Their red-hot form makes them strong contenders and a major focus for the Indian contingent.

In singles, P.V. Sindhu continues her journey back to peak form, seeking consistency and confidence on the demanding BWF Tour. Meanwhile, Lakshya Sen will be looking to build momentum after a somewhat turbulent Singapore Open outing. H.S. Prannoy, who recently secured a significant win over World No. 5 Jonatan Christie, aims to carry that positive energy forward. The women's doubles pair of Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand also return, crucial for India's Asian Games aspirations.

Beyond the familiar names, several other Indian shuttlers have shown flashes of brilliance. Kidambi Srikanth reached the US Open final, demonstrating his enduring class, while young Rounak Chouhan pulled off a stunning upset against a top-10 player at the same event. Men's singles player Rithvik recently clinched the Valence Alpes International title, and the mixed doubles pair of Tanisha Crasto and Dhruv Kapila impressed at the Singapore Open. The draw for the Japan Open is yet to be announced, but the Indian squad will be bracing for tough challenges in Tokyo.

indiaContingent.name: HS Prannoy
indiaContingent.note: Looking to build on recent wins
